audit_fixes.issue: Hang Power Clean on Day 2 + Day 5 (same explosive pattern twice)
audit_fixes.fix: Day 5 explosive changed to KB Snatch (P1) / Hang Power Snatch (P2) — different plane and pattern

audit_fixes.issue: Power Clean from Floor on Day 2 + Day 5 (P2 duplicate)
audit_fixes.fix: Same as above — Day 5 P2 now uses Hang Power Snatch

audit_fixes.issue: Incline DB Press on Day 1 secondary + Day 4 secondary
audit_fixes.fix: Day 4 secondary changed to Dumbbell Flat Press (neutral grip) — different angle, same joint-friendly principle

audit_fixes.issue: Chest-Supported Row on Day 1 Acc A + Day 4 Acc A
audit_fixes.fix: Day 4 changed to Seal Row — same stimulus, different setup. Variation is intentional

audit_fixes.issue: Face Pull on Day 1 Acc B + Day 4 Acc B
audit_fixes.fix: KEPT — face pull is rotator cuff maintenance, intentionally on every upper day. Flagged as intentional duplicate per joint longevity rules

audit_fixes.issue: Lateral Raise on Day 1 Acc B AND Day 1 Finisher (same session)
audit_fixes.fix: Day 1 finisher lateral raise removed — replaced with Band Pull-Apart only + Push-up

audit_fixes.issue: Bulgarian Split Squat on Day 2 Acc A + Day 5 secondary
audit_fixes.fix: Day 5 secondary changed to Hack Squat or Heel-Elevated Goblet Squat — different quad emphasis pattern

audit_fixes.issue: Hip Thrust on Day 2 Acc B + Day 5 Acc A
audit_fixes.fix: Day 5 changed to Cable Pull-Through — same glute hinge stimulus, different load vector

audit_fixes.issue: Single-Leg RDL on Day 2 Acc B + Day 5 Acc B
audit_fixes.fix: Day 2 changed to Nordic Curl or Lying DB Leg Curl — unilateral removed from Day 2, Day 5 keeps the unilateral RDL

audit_fixes.issue: Farmer Carry on Day 2, Day 3, Day 5, Day 6 (4x in one week)
audit_fixes.fix: Standardized: Day 2 = Farmer Carry, Day 3 = Suitcase Carry (anti-lateral, different stimulus), Day 5 = Overhead Carry, Day 6 = Farmer Carry. Each carry variant has distinct training purpose

audit_fixes.issue: Box Jump on Day 1 primer + Day 6 Circuit B
audit_fixes.fix: Day 6 changed to Depth Drop to Broad Jump — reactive emphasis, different stimulus than max height box jump

audit_fixes.issue: KB Swing in Day 2 finisher + Day 6 circuits
audit_fixes.fix: KEPT — KB Swing in Day 2 is finisher metabolic. Day 6 is Olympic primer power development. Different context, different intent. Flagged as intentional

audit_fixes.issue: Overhead Tricep Extension on Day 1 Acc B + Day 5 Acc B
audit_fixes.fix: Day 1 changed to Tricep Rope Pushdown — rope flare targets lateral tricep head. Day 5 keeps Overhead Extension for long head stretch. Different attachment, angle, and head emphasis

days.name: Heavy Upper
days.theme: Heavy Chest · Strength Back · Accessory Shoulders

P1.notes: Reset fully — every rep max effort

P2.notes: Quality over speed — same intent

P1.notes: Step down — never jump down

P2.notes: Depth jump variation — step off box first

P1.technique: 3-1-1 tempo. Week 4-5: final set to 1 RIR
P1.notes: Controlled descent every rep

P2.technique: Cluster sets wk 6-7 (2+2+2 / 15s rest). Heavy doubles wk 8-9. 2s pause at chest
P2.notes: Back-off set: 1x6-8 at 85% of top set

P1.notes: 3-1-1 tempo — full stretch at bottom

P2.notes: Neutral grip maintained — shoulder health at higher loads

P1.notes: Dead hang to chin — full ROM

P2.notes: No kipping. Controlled strength phase

P1.notes: 2-0-2 tempo. Rest-pause final set

P2.notes: Feel the stretch — maintained volume

P1.notes: 3-1-1 tempo. 1s hold at top. Rest-pause final set

P2.notes: Pulling equals pressing — track weekly

P1.notes: 2-0-2 tempo. Rest-pause final set

P2.notes: Maintenance volume in strength phase

exercises.duplicate_reason: Rotator cuff maintenance — intentionally programmed every upper day per joint longevity rules

P1.notes: Pull to forehead. Elbows high throughout

P2.notes: Rear delt + rotator cuff — non-negotiable every upper day

P1.notes: Rope attachment — flare at bottom. Different stimulus from Day 5 Overhead Extension

P2.notes: Rope flare at bottom — lat head emphasis vs long head on Day 5

exercises.audit_note: Fixed: changed from Overhead Tricep Extension (which is on Day 5) to Rope Pushdown — lateral head vs long head, distinct stimulus

P1.notes: No rest between movements

P2.notes: No rest between movements

P1.notes: 60s rest between rounds

P2.notes: 60s rest between rounds

days.mindset: You're not here because you have to be. You're here because you decided something. Most men become fathers without deciding what kind of father to be. You're deciding through action, not intention. Finish this: the father I am becoming is ___.
days.dad_mission: Write one sentence tonight: 'The father I am becoming is ___.' First honest answer. Read it before tomorrow's session.

days.name: Heavy Lower
days.theme: Squat Dominant · Posterior Chain · Loaded Carries

exercises.audit_note: Fixed: Day 5 explosive now uses KB Snatch / Hang Power Snatch — no longer duplicated

P1.notes: Catch in quarter squat — elbows high and fast. Hang variation P1

P2.notes: P2: power clean from floor. Fast elbows into quarter squat

P1.notes: Land soft — absorb through hips and knees

P2.notes: Reactive — land controlled, absorb the force

P1.notes: 3s eccentric. Week 4-5: push final set to 1 RIR

P2.notes: Cluster sets wk 6-7. Heavy doubles wk 8-9. 2s pause at bottom. Back-off: 1x6-8 at 85%

P1.notes: Full ROM — keep tension, don't lock out

P2.notes: Push load every week. Full ROM always

P1.notes: 3-1-1 tempo — feel the hamstring, not the back

P2.notes: 3-1-1 tempo. Heavier than P1 — maintain the hamstring feel

blocks.audit_note: Fixed: Bulgarian Split Squat removed from Day 5 secondary — now lives only here on Day 2

P1.notes: 3s eccentric. Rest-pause final set

P2.notes: Heavier P2 — maintain 3s eccentric. Form before load

exercises.audit_note: Fixed: replaces Single-Leg RDL which was also on Day 5. Nordic Curl = unilateral hamstring, different stimulus

P1.notes: Lower as slowly as possible — catch yourself at the bottom. Hardest hamstring exercise you can do

P2.load_guidance: Bodyweight — add band assist if needed
P2.notes: P2: aim for more reps. Eccentric control is the priority

exercises.audit_note: Fixed: Hip Thrust kept on Day 2 only. Day 5 now uses Cable Pull-Through

P1.notes: Drive through heels. 1s pause at top

P2.notes: Heavier P2. Hard squeeze at top — pause 1s

P1.notes: Pause at bottom — full Achilles stretch

P2.notes: Full ROM — Achilles longevity

blocks.audit_note: Carry standardized: Day 2 = Farmer, Day 3 = Suitcase, Day 5 = Overhead, Day 6 = Farmer

P1.notes: EMOM 10 min

P2.notes: EMOM 10 min

P1.notes: EMOM 10 min

P2.notes: P2: suitcase carry, not farmer

days.mindset: Nobody sees the foundation. Nobody films the pour. The squat you just hit — five sets, tired, all the way down — that's not Instagram content. That's the thing underneath everything else. Build it anyway.
days.dad_mission: Write down 3 things you did well as a father this week. Not aspirations. Specific moments that already happened. Own them.

days.name: Core + Conditioning

P1.notes: Conversational pace throughout. Do not replace with HIIT

P2.notes: Identical to P1 — primary lifts need recovery. Resist urge to push here

P1.notes: Ribs down — don't let hips sag

P2.notes: Same — anti-extension anchor across all phases

P1.notes: Brace hard — zero rotation

P2.notes: Increase load if P1 form was controlled

P1.notes: Full ROM — control the descent

P2.notes: Progress by slowing descent — not adding weight yet

P1.notes: Initiate from hips — upper body follows

P2.notes: Same movement — slightly more load

blocks.audit_note: Fixed: Day 3 carry changed to Suitcase Carry — anti-lateral flexion, distinct from Farmer Carry on Day 2 and Day 6

P1.notes: Walk tall — don't lean away from the weight

P2.notes: Longer distance P2

P1.notes: Slow and deliberate — exhale on extension

P2.notes: Same — deliberate. Breathe out on extension

days.mindset: This session doesn't feel like training — that's the point. The dads who last aren't the ones who never have bad days. They're the ones who keep moving on them.
days.dad_mission: Do something physical with your child or partner after this session. A 10-minute walk counts. Movement is connection.

days.name: Hypertrophy Upper + Biceps
days.theme: Heavy Shoulders · Volume Chest · Accessory Back · Biceps

P1.notes: Extend fully overhead — slam with intent

P2.notes: Aggressive slam — P2 intent

P1.notes: 3s eccentric down — explode up

P2.notes: P2: higher quality intent — consistent hand lift-off

P1.notes: Strict press — no leg drive. Core braced

P2.notes: Cluster sets wk 6-7. Heavy doubles wk 8-9. Strict press throughout. Back-off: 1x6-8 at 85%

blocks.audit_note: Fixed: Incline DB Press removed from Day 4 secondary — was also on Day 1. Replaced with DB Flat Press (neutral grip) — different angle

P1.notes: Neutral grip reduces shoulder stress — different angle from Day 1 Incline

P2.notes: Strength phase: push load. Neutral grip maintained

P1.notes: Dead hang every rep. Full ROM

P2.notes: Dead hang — non-negotiable

blocks.audit_note: Fixed: Chest-Supported Row replaced with Seal Row — same posterior stimulus, distinct variation from Day 1

P1.notes: Start neutral, rotate to pronated at top

P2.notes: Same rotation quality — heavier load

P1.notes: Lie prone on elevated bench — eliminates lower back. Full stretch at bottom

P2.notes: Pull to waist — 1s squeeze at top. Pulling matches pressing

P1.notes: Core braced — raise to shoulder height only

P2.notes: Width builder — non-negotiable

exercises.duplicate_reason: Rotator cuff maintenance — intentionally on every upper day per joint longevity rules

P1.notes: Elbows above cable line throughout

P2.notes: Rear delt + rotator cuff — every upper day

P1.notes: Full stretch at bottom — key to bicep growth. Rest-pause final set

P2.notes: Full stretch — heavier P2

P1.notes: Brachialis emphasis — no swinging

P2.notes: Neutral grip — controlled throughout

P1.notes: No rest between movements

P2.notes: No rest between movements

P1.notes: No rest between movements

P2.notes: No rest between movements

P1.notes: 30s rest between rounds

P2.notes: 30s rest between rounds

days.mindset: Protection isn't just physical. The strongest thing you can build is a home where it's safe to fail, to ask for help, to be scared. You are building that — not with speeches, but with behavior.
days.dad_mission: Tonight ask your partner: 'What's been the hardest thing for you this week?' No fixing. No advice. Just: 'I hear you.'

days.name: Hypertrophy Lower + Triceps

blocks.audit_note: Fixed: Changed from Hang Power Clean / Power Clean (duplicated from Day 2) to KB Snatch (P1) and Hang Power Snatch (P2)

P1.notes: Drive from hips — lock out overhead. Not a swing

P2.notes: P2: heavier KB. Full hip extension — lock out

P1.notes: Stand tall between reps

P2.notes: Full reset between reps

P1.notes: Brace before pull — hips drive, not back

P2.notes: Cluster sets wk 6-7. Heavy doubles/triples wk 8-9. Belt recommended. Back-off: 1x5-6 at 85%

blocks.audit_note: Fixed: Bulgarian Split Squat removed — now only on Day 2. Replaced with Hack Squat / Heel-Elevated Goblet Squat

P1.notes: Heels elevated 1-2 inches — more quad. Different pattern from Day 2 Leg Press

P2.notes: Strength phase: push load weekly. Heel elevation maintained

P1.notes: 3-1-1 tempo — hinge not a squat. Feel the hamstring load

P2.notes: 3-1-1 tempo. Hamstring health — do not rush eccentric

blocks.audit_note: Fixed: Hip Thrust removed from Day 5 — now only on Day 2. Replaced with Cable Pull-Through

P1.notes: Full stretch at bottom. Rest-pause final set

P2.notes: 3-1-2 tempo. Heavier than P1

exercises.audit_note: Fixed: replaces Hip Thrust which was on Day 2. Same glute hinge stimulus — different load vector

P1.notes: Drive hips forward — squeeze glutes at lockout. Rope between legs at bottom

P2.notes: Heavier P2. Full hip extension at top — hard glute squeeze

exercises.audit_note: Fixed: kept here on Day 5 only — was previously also on Day 2. Day 2 now uses Nordic Curl instead

P1.notes: Hip hinge — feel the hamstring stretch. Balance is secondary

P2.notes: Maintained from P1

P1.notes: Full stretch at bottom — non-negotiable. Rest-pause final set

P2.notes: Full stretch at bottom

P1.notes: Elbows at sides throughout

P2.notes: Isolation — controlled throughout

P1.notes: Full ROM — pause at bottom. Achilles health

P2.notes: Full ROM — Achilles longevity

blocks.audit_note: Carry standardized: Day 5 = Overhead Carry — shoulder stability, distinct from Farmer and Suitcase

P1.notes: No sled: KB Swing x15 + Overhead Carry. Lock the overhead position — ribs down

P2.notes: P2: more distance, heavier load

days.mindset: You pulled heavy again. The gap between what you feel and what you do is where fathers are built. Your kid isn't going to remember your deadlift. They'll remember who you were when it was hard.
days.dad_mission: Set an alarm 10 minutes before your usual bedtime. Put the phone down. Think of one moment this week where you were exactly the father you want to be. Go to sleep on that.

days.name: Athletic Conditioning

P1.notes: Full hip extension into clean — strict press

P2.notes: Hang Power Snatch P2 — fast elbows, lock out overhead

P1.notes: Initiate from hips — not just arms

P2.notes: P2: more aggressive hip drive

P1.notes: 30s on / 30s off — sprint not a fast ride

P2.notes: P2: track output and beat last week

blocks.audit_note: Fixed: Box Jump removed — was also on Day 1 primer. Replaced with Depth Drop to Broad Jump — reactive emphasis, different stimulus

exercises.duplicate_reason: KB Snatch on Day 5 is strength primer (4 reps/side, heavy, full rest). Day 6 is conditioning circuit (6 reps/side, moderate, short rest). Different intent and context

P1.notes: Full hip extension — lock out overhead

P2.notes: Full hip extension — lock out

exercises.audit_note: Fixed: replaces Box Jump which was also Day 1 primer. Reactive landing emphasis — different stimulus

P1.notes: Step off box (12-16 inch) → land → immediately broad jump. Minimize ground contact

P2.notes: P2: slightly higher step-off — more reactive challenge

P1.notes: Pull to hip → shrug → high pull — three-phase movement

P2.notes: P2: heavier — same three-phase intent

P1.notes: Drive from hips throughout

P2.notes: P2: more aggressive output

blocks.audit_note: Carry standardized: Day 6 = Farmer Carry. Intentionally same as Day 2 — this is the weekly bookend. Day 3 = Suitcase, Day 5 = Overhead keep variety across the week

P1.notes: Walk tall. Last thing you do every week — go heavy

P2.notes: P2: load should be uncomfortably heavy. Walk tall throughout

days.mindset: You finished the week. Not perfectly — nobody does. Presence is not about being perfect. It's about being there, repeatedly, over time.
days.dad_mission: Tonight: phone in another room for one hour. Be in the same room as your child or partner with no agenda. That's the whole mission.

days.name: Active Recovery

activities.notes: Low HR — get outside. Sunlight matters for recovery

activities.activity: Foam roll: quads, hamstrings, thoracic, lats
activities.notes: 60s per area — find tight spots. Breathe into them
Desk + baby-holding = tight hip flexors. Every father needs this
Spinal decompression — do every week without exception

days.mindset: Rest is not a reward for hard training. Rest is part of the training. Sustainable fatherhood looks boring from the outside. From the inside, it feels like freedom.

day_6.purpose: Weekly bookend — grip and posture finisher

intentional_duplicates.reason: Rotator cuff maintenance — programmed every upper day per joint longevity rules. Not a training stimulus duplication
Day 2 = metabolic finisher. Day 6 = power development primer. Different intensity, volume, and intent
Day 5 = 4 reps/side, heavy, 90s rest — power primer. Day 6 = 6 reps/side, moderate, circuit context. Different stimulus
